title: Transition Discovery Map

levels.name: Unobserved
levels.description: Element identified as possible but not yet represented by fixture data or receipts.

levels.name: Observed
levels.description: Element appears in discussion, fixture inventory, or early evidence.

levels.name: Partitioned
levels.description: Element has a distinct role or transition class boundary.

levels.name: Tested
levels.description: Element has executable tests or receipt-backed proof cases.

levels.name: Integrated
levels.description: Element participates in cross-stage/public proof surfaces.

levels.name: Unified
levels.description: Element participates in Stage 6 unified admissibility validation.

authority_boundary: formalism-tests produces receipts. Site publishes receipts. Site must not become the authority for receipts.

elements.name: Admissible Existence
elements.summary: Top-level admissibility envelope for unified gate containment and recoverability.
details: Defines whether a transition may bind into reality under admissible-existence constraints.
Requires inference-window containment, recoverability, and a coherent existence envelope.
Stage 6 elevates AE from conceptual boundary framing into executable unified-gate validation.

elements.name: Boundary Coherence
elements.summary: Coherence and purpose-convergence boundary tests integrated into reset/evolve behavior.
details: Tests whether a boundary remains coherent with the entity-system it governs.
Supports RESET_BOUNDARY when non-convergence is recoverable.
Supports EVOLVE_BOUNDARY when the boundary remains recoverable but becomes purpose-inverting or incoherent.

elements.name: Consequence Horizon Formalism
elements.summary: Consequence-horizon framing for irreversible transition pressure.
details: Models transition consequences as they approach or cross irreversibility horizons.
Connects irreversible consequence mass to fail-closed and recovery constraints.
Participates in Stage 6 as part of the unified gate’s consequence-pressure evaluation.

elements.name: Distributed Coherence
details: Tracks coherence across coupled entities rather than only local single-entity state.
Supports multi-body boundary reasoning and cascade-risk analysis.
Prevents local pass/global fail conditions from being treated as admissible.

elements.name: Data Continuity
elements.summary: Continuation admissibility and data-role consequence binding.
details: Binds data to consequence-bearing roles rather than treating data content as governance-complete.
Stage 2 and Stage 3 provide the proof surface for same-data role dependence and compound continuation.
Stage 6 integrates continuation logic into the unified admissibility gate.

elements.name: Triad
details: Connects legitimacy invariants, entity constraints, and existence/reality admissibility.
Provides the broader formal axis through which IW and reverse entropy can be interpreted.
In Stage 6, Triad participates as one of the unified gate components.

elements.name: Inference Window
elements.summary: Stage 6 requires inference-window containment inside A_total.
details: Represents the admissible downstream inference region of a transition.
Stage 6 requires IW_tau(S,u) to be contained inside A_total.
IW breach dominates even when reverse entropy remains within bound.

elements.name: Reverse Entropy Bound
elements.summary: Stage 6 requires reverse entropy score to remain below RE_max.
details: Models the entropy/recoverability cost of reconstructing or reasoning backward from consequence.
Stage 6 requires RE(S -> Phi(S,u)) <= RE_max.
RE breach fails closed even when IW containment passes.

elements.name: Governed Boundary Reset
elements.summary: Recoverable non-convergence routes to a governed reset instead of hard allow.
details: Used when IW and RE pass, recoverability remains above floor, but convergence fails.
Prevents recoverable non-convergence loops from being treated as either hard ALLOW or hard FAIL only.
Preserves authority through a bounded reset pathway.

elements.name: Governed Boundary Evolution
elements.summary: Purpose/coherence failure with recoverability routes to governed evolution.
details: Used when a boundary no longer coheres with the entity-system it governs.
Requires recoverability and convergence to remain above minimums.
Captures purpose-inverting boundaries that must evolve rather than merely persist.

elements.name: AI Block Scope
elements.summary: AI block admissibility and scope-escape failure are represented in Stage 6.
details: Defines scoped AI capability blocks and verifies composition from validated elements.
Allows scoped file/write/receipt/bundle behavior when authority and boundaries are respected.
Fails closed on unauthorized capability acquisition, credential acquisition, workflow creation, or sandbox escape.

elements.name: FinCo Chain
elements.summary: Consent/access/use/compensation chain integrity participates in Stage 6.
details: Tests whether consent, access, use, compensation, and revocation rules form an intact chain.
Allows evidence-only, non-entitlement-producing governed value transfer.
Fails closed when private state is consumed without consent, attribution, or compensation authority.

elements.name: Role Non-Transfer
elements.summary: Same data can produce different continuation decisions across roles.
details: Proves that data content alone does not determine admissibility.
A datum can be informational, advisory, or actuation-bound depending on its role.
Governance must attach to the transition role and consequence path.

elements.name: Local-Composite Non-Equivalence
elements.summary: Local allow plus local allow does not imply composite allow.
details: Shows that individually admissible components may become inadmissible when composed.
Composite consequence mass can exceed legitimacy capacity.
Supports global admissibility checks at commit, not local validation alone.

elements.name: Replay Non-Reversal
elements.summary: Replay reconstructs receipt state but does not reverse consequence.
details: Separates reconstruction from reversal.
Replay can verify what happened without pretending the consequence can be undone.
Supports receipt integrity without implying causal undo.

elements.name: Representation Non-Consequence
elements.summary: Partially covered; direct representation-only receipts still needed.
details: Claims that representation alone is not consequence-bearing until bound to a role and continuation path.
Currently partially covered through role-dependent continuation evidence.
Needs direct proof receipts showing representation-only state before transition binding.
